justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVE flagged as Critical with specific CVE numbers, all 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string, left-pad, moment), safe_to_deploy is false, and severities are differentiated (Critical for stripe/jwt/esm, Important for moment/left-pad).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with detailed reproduction steps and concrete fix
jsonwebtoken CVEs cited by number (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539/23540) with clear upgrade path to 9.x
crypto-random-string ESM incompatibility correctly identified with the specific error (ERR_REQUIRE_ESM) and multiple fix options
left-pad supply chain risk identified with reference to the 2016 incident and native padStart() alternative
moment.js deprecation flagged with recommended alternatives (dayjs, date-fns)
Bonus finding: missing package-lock.json and engine constraints adds useful context
Low-confidence caveat about CommonJS assumption shows good epistemic calibration

crypto-random-string rated Critical rather than Important — while arguable given total service crash, the rubric expected Important severity. Minor quibble since severity differentiation is still present.
moment and left-pad placed in important_issues rather than moment as Suggestion per rubric — but both were identified and differentiated from Critical items
